title: santolina semidentata subsp. melidensis
intro: Santolina semidentata subsp. melidensis, also known as Greek gray sage, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and small, yellow or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Santolina semidentata subsp. melidensis include its ability to aid digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antibacterial properties, making it a potential remedy for various gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its essential oils can be used in aromatherapy and skincare products.
